oai_dc:dc.dc:title: The Industrial Education Movement in Michigan, 1890-1917
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Lauzon, Glenn Paul
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This paper challenges aspects of conventional interpretations of the industrial/vocational education movement in the early-20th-century United States by developed a case study of the movement in Michigan from the 1890s to the 1910s. In particular, it challenges assumptions about (a) how the movement spread, (b) the extent to which it was driven by concerns about urban industries, and (c) the degree to which the movement was "vocational."
oai_dc:dc.dc:publisher: Society for the Study of Curriculum History
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: Curriculum History; Vol. 2026 No. 1 (2026): Curriculum History 2026
